name: Salt Lake (Bidhannagar)
description: Salt Lake City, officially known as Bidhannagar, is the IT hub of Kolkata located in North 24 Parganas. Home to Sector V IT Park — the largest IT hub in eastern India — Salt Lake is a planned township with wide roads, organized sectors, and excellent connectivity. Major companies like TCS, Wipro, Cognizant, Infosys, and Capgemini have offices here. Salt Lake is also home to Nalban Food Park, Nicco Park, Central Park, and the Kolkata Book Fair grounds at Milan Mela Prangan.
hospitals: Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Cancer Hospital

name: New Town (Rajarhat)
description: New Town, also known as Rajarhat, is Kolkata's fastest-growing satellite township. Known for its modern infrastructure, wide roads, and planned development, New Town is home to Eco Park — one of India's largest urban parks — as well as Biswa Bangla Convention Centre, Tata Consultancy Services campus, and the upcoming Kolkata Eye hospital. New Town houses multiple IT parks, residential complexes, and is becoming Kolkata's corporate hub with offices of Wipro, HCL, and Accenture.
keywords: cab near me new town kolkata

name: Howrah
description: Howrah is Kolkata's twin city located across the Hooghly River, connected by the iconic Howrah Bridge (Rabindra Setu). Howrah Junction is one of India's busiest and oldest railway stations, serving millions of passengers annually. The city is also home to the Botanical Garden with its famous 250-year-old Great Banyan Tree, Belur Math (headquarters of Ramakrishna Mission), and the Howrah Maidan. Howrah is a major transit point for travelers heading to and from Kolkata by train.

name: Park Street
description: Park Street, officially known as Mother Teresa Sarani, is the cultural and nightlife hub of Kolkata. Famous for its colonial architecture, restaurants, pubs, and shopping, Park Street is often called the 'Food Street of Kolkata'. It houses some of the city's most iconic establishments — Flurys, Peter Cat, Mocambo, and Trincas. Park Street is also close to major landmarks like St. Paul's Cathedral, Victoria Memorial, Indian Museum, and the Maidan. It's a prime area for business travelers and tourists.

name: Dum Dum (Airport Area)
description: Dum Dum is a historic area in North Kolkata, best known as the location of Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose International Airport (CCU). The area derives its name from the Dum Dum Arsenal, a British-era ammunition factory. Today, Dum Dum is a major transit hub with excellent connectivity to the rest of Kolkata via Metro, bus, and auto. It's the first point of contact for air travelers arriving in Kolkata, making reliable cab service essential for airport transfers to all parts of the city.
keywords: dum dum to salt lake cab

name: Ballygunge
description: Ballygunge is an upscale residential neighborhood in South Kolkata known for its tree-lined avenues, heritage buildings, and proximity to the Maidan. It's one of Kolkata's most sought-after residential areas with excellent educational institutions like St. Xavier's College, Loreto College, and Presidency University nearby. Ballygunge is well-connected by metro (Rabindra Sadan, Jatin Das Park stations) and is close to major shopping hubs like Quest Mall, Forum Mall, and Gariahat Market.

name: Tollygunge
description: Tollygunge, fondly called 'Tolly', is a vibrant area in South Kolkata famous for the Tollygunge Club and as the home of the Bengali film industry (Tollywood). Located along the Metro corridor, Tollygunge offers excellent connectivity to the entire city. The area is home to Rabindra Sarobar (the city's largest lake-park), Nalban Park, and the historic Tollygunge Racecourse. It's a mix of old Kolkata charm and modern residential development, making it one of the most livable areas in the city.

name: Barasat
description: Barasat is a major suburban city and the district headquarters of North 24 Parganas, located about 25 km north of central Kolkata. It serves as a crucial gateway for travelers heading towards the Sundarbans, Basirhat, and other northern destinations. Barasat has excellent road connectivity via Jessore Road (NH12) and BT Road. The area is experiencing rapid development with new residential projects and commercial complexes, making it an important pickup and drop location for cab services.

name: Behala
description: Behala is a densely populated residential area in southwestern Kolkata. It's known for Behala Chowrasta (the main intersection), Behala Flying Club, and proximity to the Joka Metro line. Behala connects to central Kolkata via Diamond Harbour Road and James Long Sarani. The area has grown significantly with residential apartments, schools, and local markets. For outstation travelers from Behala, cab service is essential as auto and bus connectivity to the airport and railway stations is limited.

name: Esplanade (BBD Bagh)
description: Esplanade, along with BBD Bagh (formerly Dalhousie Square), forms the historic government and business district of Kolkata. It's home to the Writers' Building, Kolkata High Court, General Post Office, and Reserve Bank of India. The Esplanade bus terminus is the largest bus terminal in the city, connecting Kolkata to all suburban and outstation destinations. This area is a major transit hub for business travelers, government visitors, and tourists exploring colonial Kolkata.

name: Gariahat
description: Gariahat is one of South Kolkata's most popular shopping and residential areas. The Gariahat Market and Gariahat crossing are bustling commercial zones famous for clothing, handicrafts, and local street food. The area is well-connected by bus, tram, and auto-rickshaw. Nearby landmarks include South City Mall, Golpark, Dhakuria Lake, and Lake Mall. Gariahat serves as a key residential hub for families and professionals working in the southern parts of Kolkata.

name: Jadavpur
description: Jadavpur is a vibrant area in South Kolkata, best known for Jadavpur University — one of India's most prestigious educational institutions. The area is a mix of academic, residential, and commercial spaces. Jadavpur 8B Bus Stand is a major transit point connecting south Kolkata to the rest of the city. The area is home to numerous cafes, bookshops, and cultural spaces catering to the student community. It's well-connected by bus, auto, and the upcoming Metro extension.
